headline: QQQ vs. VGT: The Best Way to Own Big Tech?
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) and the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (NYSEARCA:VGT) are often shoved into the same “own Big Tech” bucket, but they answer different questions. QQQ tracks the Nasdaq-100, a listing-based index that is growth-heavy. VGT tracks the MSCI US Investable Market Information Technology 25/50 Index, a pure GICS sector fund. That distinction ... QQQ vs. VGT: The Best Way to Own Big Tech?

headline: Mastercard Inc (MA) Secures New York Crypto License
summary: Mastercard Inc (NYSE:MA) is one of the most profitable crypto-exposed stocks to buy now. On May 27, Mastercard Inc (NYSE:MA) announced that it has been granted a BitLicense in New York. The company cast this approval as a boost to its digital asset business ambitions, saying it demonstrates its commitment to meeting high regulatory standards. […]

headline: What the $38 billion Visa, Mastercard swipe fee settlement means for credit card users
summary: A judge has preliminarily approved a $38 billion Visa-Mastercard settlement with merchants over swipe fees. If finalized, it could affect card acceptance, surcharges, and merchant costs.

headline: Mastercard Faces US$38b Fee Settlement While Shares Flagged As Undervalued
summary: Visa and Mastercard, including NYSE:MA, have received preliminary court approval for a US$38b settlement with U.S. merchants over alleged excessive credit card processing fees. The settlement includes fee reductions for merchants over five years and a ceiling on interchange rates for eight years. This legal development follows decades of disputes between card networks and merchants over the cost structure of card based transactions. For Mastercard, whose core business centers on processing...

headline: Europe’s Payments Leaders Gather in London as Control, Fraud and AI Reshape the Industry
summary: OMAHA, Neb., June 10, 2026--As Europe’s payments industry enters a decisive phase marked by sovereignty pressures, new regulatory requirements and the rise of AI‑driven commerce, the industry’s most influential players will convene in London later this month to debate who controls the rails, bears the risk and shapes the future.
